feedback.text: My name is Toby and I run a small game studio in Sweden. Our art form, game development, is quite young but already thousands of works have been erased from history. Not necessarily out of malice but out of carelessness. This is a sad fact as without preservation part of the history of the art form is lost. However, over the past couple of decades many games have been released with sort of a built in kill switch that when activated renders the game unplayable. As a developer this is rarely our choice, but rather the decision of big publishers and the idea of erasure of my life's work is devastating. Hopefully this initiative will bring into question the legality of destroying games that have been sold to customers and I, as a developer, is very much in favour of that.
